About the Role

We are seeking a qualified capital/finance professional to lead the capital and control workstream for new regulated insurance entities established by the Group — including DFSA, Cayman, and Lloyd’s structures. The primary focus of the role is on new entity establishment: defining the capital structure, building the financial control framework, and establishing the regulatory reporting infrastructure from inception. Once entities are operational, you will support their ongoing financial and regulatory requirements, alongside existing entities and reinsurance programs. You will also supervise a junior analyst. This is a build-focused role for someone comfortable standing things up from scratch, not just running steady-state reporting.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the financial establishment of new regulated entities (DFSA, Cayman, Lloyd’s) — defining the opening balance sheet, capital structure, and financial control framework from inception.
  • Build capital adequacy and solvency models for new entities to meet regulatory requirements; maintain and update these models as entities grow.
  • Establish regulatory reporting frameworks for new jurisdictions (e.g., DFSA, Cayman, Lloyd’s), including designing reporting packs, setting calendars, and producing initial returns.

  • Reconcile intercompany balances and positions across group entities.
  • Manage the Group’s Funds at Lloyd’s (FAL) position for Syndicate in a Box (SIAB), including capital setting processes, FAL composition optimization, and capital calls.
  • Run capital adequacy stress testing and scenario analysis across regulated entities to assess headroom above SCR/MCR thresholds.
  • Manage collateral arrangements (letters of credit, trust accounts) for reinsurance programs.
  • Own capital waterfall and inter-entity funding mechanics, ensuring compliance with approved limits.
  • Track and report reinsurance counterparty credit exposure, maintaining a counterparty register.
  • Produce board-level capital reporting packs (capital dashboard, solvency headroom, FAL position) quarterly or as required.
  • Support existing entities and reinsurance programs with financial control, reporting, or capital queries.
  • Supervise and direct the Capital & Structuring Analyst on day-to-day work.

  • Hands-on financial modeling experience, ideally in insurance/reinsurance.
  • Understanding of reinsurance accounting (quota share, excess of loss) and collateral management.
  • Demonstrable experience setting up financial/reporting infrastructure for regulated entities in DFSA/DIFC, Cayman, or Lloyd’s.
  • Strong Xero skills and advanced Excel/modeling ability.
  • Comfortable working independently.

Contact: jobs@yarabltd.co.uk

job_description:
About Us

Way.com — America’s dominant automotive super app, powering every mile for over 10 million customers—from auto insurance and parking to EV charging and more. Leveraging cutting-edge AI, machine learning, and advanced data analytics, we deliver innovative, personalized solutions that transform car ownership. Featured by Bloomberg and ranked 48th on Andreessen Horowitz’s Marketplace list of fastest-growing companies worldwide and recognized as the top Product in Insurance by UnitQ, we’re making car ownership easier, more affordable, and more rewarding.

Job Title

SEO Content Writer

Work Arrangement

On-site

Employment Type

Full-time

Role Overview

We are seeking a Content Writer to research, write, and publish high-quality content that drives organic traffic and supports Way’s growth in the U.S. market. This is a writing-first role with a strong SEO backbone. You will produce content that ranks, reads well, and moves consumers to action across Way’s product verticals: car wash, parking, gas, insurance, and roadside assistance. You will collaborate closely with the SEO & Content Manager to align on editorial priorities, keyword targets, and content performance.

Key Responsibilities

  • Blog & Long-Form Content: Write original, well-researched blog posts and articles targeting specific keywords and search intent while maintaining a natural, engaging voice. Maintain a consistent publishing cadence aligned with the editorial calendar.
  • Web Copy & Landing Pages: Write and refine website copy, product pages, and landing pages optimized for search. Craft meta titles, descriptions, and headers to improve click-through rates. Support new page creation and site updates with SEO-informed copy.
  • SEO Content Execution: Collaborate on keyword research, topic clustering, and content briefs. Write content structured for on-page SEO (heading hierarchy, internal linking, schema-friendly formatting). Update existing content to improve rankings and relevance.
  • Research & Subject Matter Development: Develop expertise in Way’s product verticals and the U.S. consumer landscape. Research competitors, industry trends, and consumer questions to identify content opportunities. Translate complex topics (insurance, EV charging, parking regulations) into clear, accessible content.
  • Editorial Quality & Brand Voice: Write in a consistent brand voice that is clear, helpful, and confident. Self-edit rigorously for clarity, grammar, structure, and readability. Incorporate feedback to improve future output.

Preferred Skills & Requirements

  • 3–5 years of experience as a content writer, copywriter, or similar role producing web content.
  • Strong portfolio of published blog posts, articles, or web copy with demonstrable SEO results.
  • Solid understanding of on-page SEO principles (keyword placement, heading structure, internal linking, meta tags).
  • Experience writing for U.S. audiences with a natural command of American English idiom and tone.
  • Ability to research and write confidently across unfamiliar topics.
  • Experience working with content management systems (WordPress or similar).
  • Familiarity with SEO tools (Ahrefs, SEMrush, Google Search Console).
  • Strong self-editing skills with publish-ready first drafts.
  • Ability to manage multiple content assignments and meet deadlines.
  • Comfortable working with a U.S.-based content calendar and cross-timezone collaboration.

What We Look For

  • Clear thinking and simplicity: Make complex topics easy to understand.
  • Ownership and accountability: See content through from research to publish.
  • Speed with quality: Produce volume without sacrificing craft.
  • Reader-first writing: Create useful content that also ranks.
  • Curiosity over formula: Dig into topics to understand them deeply.

What This Role Is (and Isn’t)

This is a writing role focused on researching, writing, and publishing content—not managing teams or setting strategy. The SEO & Content Manager sets priorities; you bring them to life. If you’re seeking a management role, this isn’t it. If you want to write content reaching millions of consumers and improve your craft weekly, this is the role.

Why You Will Love Working with Us

  • Opportunity to work for the #1 Auto Super app in the US.
  • Impact across 10+ million users.
  • Dynamic, fast-paced, innovation-driven culture.
  • Clear career growth in a rapidly scaling tech company.
  • Competitive compensation.

Apply Now

  • Address: 4th Floor, Yamuna, Technopark Phase-III, Thiruvananthapuram, 695583
  • Contact Email: careers@way.com

  • Manage Android/iOS build processes, testing, and deployments.
  • Utilize tools like Git, CI/CD pipelines, and testing suites.
  • Optimize app performance and ensure high-quality user experiences.
  • Participate in sprints, code reviews, debugging, and technical discussions.

Required Skills & Qualifications

  • Strong hands-on experience in React Native or similar frameworks.
  • Solid understanding of JavaScript/TypeScript (ES6+) and mobile architecture.
  • Familiarity with state management libraries (Redux, Recoil, Zustand, Context API).
  • RESTful API and cloud service integration (e.g., Firebase).
  • Basic backend knowledge (Node.js, Express.js, NoSQL databases).
  • Git and version control proficiency.
  • Mobile deployment processes for Google Play Store and Apple App Store.

Preferred Skills

  • Experience with Expo and native module integration.
  • UI/UX design conversion from wireframes/Figma.
  • Relational databases (PostgreSQL/MySQL).
  • Analytics tools (PostHog/Sentry).

Educational Qualification

Bachelor’s or Master’s in Computer Science/IT or equivalent experience.

Technology Ecosystem

React Native • TypeScript • Redux • Firebase • Node.js • Express.js • MongoDB • REST APIs • Git • CI/CD Pipelines
summary: Seeking a Mobile App Developer with 3-4 years of experience in cross-platform frameworks like React Native. Responsibilities include building high-performance apps, API integrations, and collaborating with teams for seamless user experiences.
